mnemonics.title: SARA - Symbolic AI Rules Always
mnemonics.content: SARA reminds you: Symbolic AI Relies on Explicit Rules and Algorithms. Think: 'SARA follows the rulebook strictly, no guessing!'

mnemonics.title: D.E.L.T.A. - Data Enables Learning Through Analysis
mnemonics.content: Data-Driven AI: DELTA learns patterns from Data, uses Examples to learn, looks for Trends, adjusts its Algorithms. Like a student who learns from past exam papers!

mnemonics.title: The Chef Analogy
mnemonics.content: Symbolic AI = Chef with a written recipe book (explicit rules). Data-Driven AI = Chef who tastes thousands of dishes and learns what makes food good (learns from data).

acronyms.usage: Used to refer to traditional Symbolic AI based on logic and rules, popular before the ML boom
Used in Symbolic AI to store facts and rules that the system can reason with
Core of Data-Driven AI; systems that learn from data without being explicitly programmed
Subset of ML using neural networks with many layers; purely data-driven approach

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a flowchart or decision tree with boxes connected by arrows. Each box contains an 'IF-THEN' rule like 'IF it rains THEN take an umbrella.' Everything is visible and follows a path.

Picture a neural network - many circles (neurons) connected by lines. Data flows in from the left, gets transformed through layers, and output comes out right. It's like a 'black box' - you see input-output but not exact rules.

Draw two columns: Left side = Rule Book (Symbolic) - clear steps, like GPS instructions. Right side = Practice Makes Perfect (Data-Driven) - like learning to play cricket by watching and mimicking players.

analogies.analogy: A GPS Navigation System: You enter a destination, and the GPS follows pre-programmed rules and maps to give turn-by-turn directions. It doesn't 'learn' from driving - it uses explicit logic.

A Child Learning to Speak: A child doesn't learn grammar rules first; they listen to thousands of examples, recognize patterns, and gradually learn to speak. Data-Driven AI works the same way - learns from massive examples!

Symbolic AI is like a calculator (fixed operations: + - × ÷). Data-Driven AI is like a camera that learns to recognize faces - it sees many faces and learns what makes a 'face' without explicit rules.
